quick_skim.title: ToolFailBench: Diagnosing Tool-Use Failures in LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Tool calling is central to modern language model agents, but aggregate benchmark scores often hide where tool use fails. A model that never calls a needed tool and a model that calls the tool but ignores the result can look similar under final task accuracy. We introduce ToolFailBench, a diagnostic benchmark for measuring tool-use failures across 1,000 tasks in finance, medicine, law, cybersecurity, and real estate. Tool-required tasks return values the model wouldn't guess, forcing it to trust the tool while control tasks attach the same tools but should be answered directly. We label each trace with Tool-Skip, Result-Ignore, Output-Fabrication, and Unnecessary-Tool-Use, using a rule classifier and two LLM judges aggregated by majority vote. Across 19 headline models, the best reaches 86.33% Clean Tool-Use Rate, showing that faithful tool use is not saturated. More importantly, models with similar aggregate scores fail in different ways: most stay disciplined on no-tool controls, while Llama-3.1 models show an Always-Call pattern, and at the same parameter scale Llama-3.1-70B and Qwen2.5-72B differ by 89 percentage points on control-task accuracy. Tool-use evaluation should measure not only whether agents call tools, but whether they use tool outputs correctly and avoid tools when none is needed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diagnostic benchmark for tool-use failures in LLM agents, includes medical domain tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM智能体工具使用失败诊断基准，包含医学领域任务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM智能体工具使用失败诊断基准，包含医学领域任务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM智能体工具使用失败诊断基准，包含医学领域任务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ToolFailBench, a diagnostic benchmark covering 1,000 tasks across five domains including medicine, to identify fine-grained tool-use failures in LLM agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出ToolFailBench，一个诊断LLM智能体工具使用失败的基准，覆盖金融、医学、法律、网络安全和房地产五个领域的1000个任务。通过标记工具跳过、结果忽略、输出伪造和多余工具使用四种失败类型，可精准定位智能体缺陷。医学领域任务的存在使其直接适用于医疗智能体评估，但未使用EHR数据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出ToolFailBench，一个诊断LLM智能体工具使用失败的基准，覆盖金融、医学、法律、网络安全和房地产五个领域的1000个任务。通过标记工具跳过、结果忽略、输出伪造和多余工具使用四种失败类型，可精准定位智能体缺陷。医学领域任务的存在使其直接适用于医疗智能体评估，但未使用EHR数据。

quick_skim.title: Between Knowledge and Care: A Mixed-Methods Evaluation of Generative AI for T2DM Self-Management from Patient and Physician Perspectives
quick_skim.abstract: Generative AI is increasingly used for everyday health guidance, yet its clinical appropriateness in chronic disease contexts remains poorly understood. This paper presents a two-part mixed-methods study on \revise{Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM)}, examining how patients and physicians assess AI-generated health information. \revise{Study~1} analyzes 784 \revise{participant reported} patient queries to characterize seven informational need categories and \revise{develops a structured five dimensional physician rating rubric informed by patient query categories and clinician priorities} (\textit{Accuracy, Safety, Clarity, Integrity, Action Orientation}). \revise{Study~2} engages seven physicians scoring responses from four AI models and discussing evaluative reasoning through in-depth interviews. Models perform well on factual explanation and lifestyle guidance but consistently underperform on medication reasoning and emotional support. Two \revise{analytic concepts} emerge \revise{from the data}. The \textit{pre-visit primer} \revise{frames AI as preparation for clinical encounters rather than as a replacement for physicians}. The \textit{fluency illusion} \revise{describes how polished language may convey epistemic authority that the clinical content does not support}. Patients and physicians converged on three shared limitations (role boundaries, emotional inadequacy, personalization gaps) while diverging in evaluative emphasis, \revise{which informed} four design directions, task-aware orchestration, risk-aware fallback, dynamic personalization, and emotionally attuned interaction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Develops a physician rating rubric for evaluating generative AI health guidance, providing evaluation methodology for medical AI agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 开发了用于评估生成式AI健康指导的医生评分量规，为医疗AI代理评估提供方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 开发了用于评估生成式AI健康指导的医生评分量规，为医疗AI代理评估提供方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 开发了用于评估生成式AI健康指导的医生评分量规，为医疗AI代理评估提供方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Mixed-methods study analyzing 784 patient queries to develop a five-dimensional physician rating rubric (Accuracy, Safety, Clarity, Integrity, Action Orientation) for evaluating generative AI in T2DM self-management, contributing to medical agent evaluation metrics
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 慢性病场景下生成式AI的临床适宜性尚不明确。本文开展混合方法研究，分析了784个患者查询，归纳出七类信息需求，并开发了包含准确性、安全性、清晰性、完整性、行动导向五个维度的医生评分量规。研究为评估医疗AI代理在真实患者咨询中的表现提供了可操作的评估量表和指标。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 慢性病场景下生成式AI的临床适宜性尚不明确。本文开展混合方法研究，分析了784个患者查询，归纳出七类信息需求，并开发了包含准确性、安全性、清晰性、完整性、行动导向五个维度的医生评分量规。研究为评估医疗AI代理在真实患者咨询中的表现提供了可操作的评估量表和指标。

quick_skim.title: Mining Workflow Graphs for Black-Box Boundary Testing of Conversational LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Conversational LLM agents can cause real-world harm when their internal workflows fail, such as completing a transaction without confirmation. Testing these state-dependent failures is difficult because critical boundaries, such as identity checks and confirmation gates, are hidden behind multi-turn conversational prerequisites, rendering them inaccessible to standard tests. We present AgentEval, a black-box testing framework that discovers and stresses these stateful boundaries. AgentEval interacts with an agent to mine a \emph{conversational workflow graph}, a model of its behavior. Instead of prompting blindly, AgentEval uses this graph's structure to enumerate specific guards and prerequisites as test targets, replaying the conversational path to a boundary before applying a perturbation. AgentEval then executes each test, determining whether it passes or fails using only the conversation turns. We benchmark AgentEval against a privileged, white-box auditor with access to the agent's underlying source code, which AgentEval never sees. On four $τ^3$-bench agents, AgentEval successfully generates tests covering $23$--$38$ distinct boundaries per agent; ablation studies attribute the gain to the graph's structure: $23$ distinct boundaries versus $12$ with a prompt-only baseline, at lower duplicate and false-alarm rates.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Black-box boundary testing framework for conversational LLM agents, directly applicable to medical agent benchmarking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对话LLM智能体黑盒边界测试框架，可直接用于医疗智能体基准测试
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对话LLM智能体黑盒边界测试框架，可直接用于医疗智能体基准测试
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对话LLM智能体黑盒边界测试框架，可直接用于医疗智能体基准测试
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentEval mines conversational workflow graphs to discover and stress stateful boundaries of LLM agents, enabling effective black-box testing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文提出AgentEval黑盒测试框架，通过与智能体交互挖掘对话工作流图，枚举状态守卫与前提条件作为测试目标，回放对话以触发边界失败。该方法能发现隐藏的状态相关失败，如未经确认的交易。虽然示例是通用对话，其方法论可直接迁移至医疗智能体的行为测试与评估。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该文提出AgentEval黑盒测试框架，通过与智能体交互挖掘对话工作流图，枚举状态守卫与前提条件作为测试目标，回放对话以触发边界失败。该方法能发现隐藏的状态相关失败，如未经确认的交易。虽然示例是通用对话，其方法论可直接迁移至医疗智能体的行为测试与评估。

quick_skim.title: HAS-Bench: Evaluating LLM-Based Human-Agent Systems under Configurable Human Participation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models increasingly operate in settings where humans are active collaborators rather than passive task providers. We introduce HAS-Framework, a graph-based framework that represents humans and LLM-powered agents as first-class participants with explicit roles, permissions, communication paths, and action authority. Building on this framework, HAS-Bench evaluates Human-Agent Systems under configurable human participation across agency levels, interaction channels, and persona policies. The benchmark measures both task outcomes and process-level collaboration behavior, including clarification quality, feedback utilization, control calibration, safety, initiative, and interaction cost. Experiments across six domains show that human participation can substantially improve task completion and failure recovery, but the gains depend on when, how, and by whom human input is exercised.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Benchmark for evaluating LLM-based human-agent systems, applicable to medical agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估LLM-Based人机协作系统的基准，可扩展到医疗agent评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估LLM-Based人机协作系统的基准，可扩展到医疗agent评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估LLM-Based人机协作系统的基准，可扩展到医疗agent评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces HAS-Framework and HAS-Bench for evaluating human-agent systems under configurable human participation across multiple domains.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM-agent评估忽略人机协作场景。本文提出HAS-Framework图式框架，将人类和LLM agent视为一阶参与者，并在此基础上构建HAS-Bench基准，支持可配置的人类参与层级、交互渠道和人物策略，评测任务结果和过程协作行为。在六个领域上的实验展示了框架的有效性。该基准可迁移至医疗领域的人机协作agent评估，如结合EHR的临床决策支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有LLM-agent评估忽略人机协作场景。本文提出HAS-Framework图式框架，将人类和LLM agent视为一阶参与者，并在此基础上构建HAS-Bench基准，支持可配置的人类参与层级、交互渠道和人物策略，评测任务结果和过程协作行为。在六个领域上的实验展示了框架的有效性。该基准可迁移至医疗领域的人机协作agent评估，如结合EHR的临床决策支持。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于EHR的医疗Agent评估, especially work that connects or combines: medical agent that uses electronic health records; benchmarking performance of medical agents; electronic health records for medical agent; medical AI agents evaluation using EHR data; reinforcement learning for healthcare agent evaluation; evaluation of clinical decision support agents with EHR; What are the metrics for healthcare agent evaluation using EHR data?; How to evaluate the performance of medical agents using electronic health records; Benchmarks for clinical decision support agents based on EHR; Reinforcement learning and LLM in medical agent evaluation.

quick_skim.title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: The rapid development of large language models and multimodal large language models has accelerated the emergence of proactive agents capable of operating everyday tools and assisting users in real-world environments. However, existing benchmarks struggle to evaluate such agents effectively, as they often rely on sandboxed environments and single-turn evaluation paradigms. Moreover, their scenario-based task taxonomies mix multiple model capabilities within the same task category, making it difficult to identify the root causes of agent failures. To address these limitations, we introduce UniClawBench, the first capability-driven benchmark designed to evaluate proactive agents in dynamic, real-world settings. UniClawBench is built around five foundational model capabilities: Skill Usage, Exploration, Long-Context Reasoning, Multimodal Understanding, and Cross-Platform Coordination. Based on these capabilities, we design 400 bilingual real-world tasks. Unlike previous benchmarks that rely on static, pre-recorded answers, our benchmark evaluates agents in live Docker containers using fine-grained, step-by-step completion checkpoints. Furthermore, we design a closed-loop evaluation strategy comprising an executor agent, a hidden supervisor agent, and a user agent to simulate realistic multi-turn human feedback without leaking grading criteria. To disentangle base model capabilities from framework-level design choices, we evaluate state-of-the-art models under multiple agent frameworks. Through comprehensive comparisons across both models and frameworks, we show how base model capabilities and agent framework designs jointly shape performance in real-world environments. To facilitate future research, we make our benchmark and code publicly available at https://github.com/HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: universal benchmark for proactive agents, transferable to medical agent evaluation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用主动智能体基准，可迁移至医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用主动智能体基准，可迁移至医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用主动智能体基准，可迁移至医疗智能体评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: First capability-driven benchmark for proactive agents in dynamic real-world settings, addressing limitations of sandboxed evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出UniClawBench，首个基于能力驱动的主动智能体基准，在动态真实环境中评估智能体。该基准围绕五个核心能力构建，克服了现有沙盒评估的局限性。虽然不直接针对医疗领域，但其方法和评估范式可迁移用于构建基于EHR的医疗智能体评估基准。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出UniClawBench，首个基于能力驱动的主动智能体基准，在动态真实环境中评估智能体。该基准围绕五个核心能力构建，克服了现有沙盒评估的局限性。虽然不直接针对医疗领域，但其方法和评估范式可迁移用于构建基于EHR的医疗智能体评估基准。
